title: R79 Finds Its Direction
text:
The destination was never the goal.
The understanding gained along the journey was always the reward.

R79 began as a search for better wheel settings.
It evolved into a search for better racing.
It continues as a search for better understanding.

Today the project moved beyond a setup app.
It is becoming a racing intelligence platform designed to help drivers learn, analyse and improve.

title: The Vision Expands
text:
Today R79 took another step beyond its original purpose.

What began as a search for better wheel settings has evolved into a racing intelligence platform.

The project is no longer simply collecting information. It is transforming information into understanding.

Every feature added now has one purpose:
to help drivers make better decisions and continuously improve.

The journey continues.

title: The Archive Was Completed
text:
Today R79 gained its memory.

The Archive has become the permanent record of the project's journey, preserving its ideas, philosophy and evolution.

From this point forward, every milestone, every lesson and every breakthrough will be documented.

The software will evolve.
The data will grow.
The technology will change.

But the purpose remains unchanged:

To help another driver find another tenth.

The journey continues.

title: AI Race Engineer Begins
text:
Today R79 began building its AI Race Engineer.

The goal is simple:
to help drivers prepare smarter before they race.

The first version brings recommendations for cars, strategy, brake balance, wheel settings, pit windows and race-specific warnings.

This is the beginning of R79 becoming more than a database.

It is becoming a digital race engineer.

Let's go.
Let's do it.
Let it be so.

The journey continues.

title: A Platform Begins to Emerge
text:
Today R79 reached an important milestone.

The project now contains its own Archive, Captain's Log, Legacy, Labs, Development Mantra and the foundations of an AI Race Engineer.

What started as a search for better wheel settings has become something much larger.

It is evolving into a racing intelligence platform designed to help drivers learn, improve and understand racing through data and AI-assisted insight.

The build completed successfully.

Another lap finished.

Another lesson learned.

Let's go.

Let's do it.

Let it be so.

The journey continues.

title: R79 Comes Alive
text:
Today R79 crossed a line that cannot be uncrossed.

What began as a search for better wheel settings has become something much greater.

The AI Race Engineer, Archive, Labs, Pathfinder, Membership, Promise and branding all came together into a single platform with its own identity and purpose.

After overcoming Git, GitHub, OneDrive and Vercel deployment issues, the latest build finally went live.

For the first time, R79 did not feel like a project.

It felt alive.

The philosophy remains unchanged:

Race smarter.
Learn faster.
Never stop improving.

And as the splash screen now reminds every visitor:

LET'S GO.
LET'S DO IT.
LET IT BE SO.

Snapshot (live project state):

- Cars indexed: 100
- Tracks supported: 42
- Games supported: 2
- AI modules: 9
- Status: Live on Vercel

Reflection:

Today was never really about code.

It was about creating a place where racers can learn, improve and help one another.

The journey continues.

title: The Alpine Incident
text:
Today R79 reached another milestone.

The application continued to evolve with new branding, Pathfinder integration, community reporting tools and database refinement. During testing an incorrect recommendation appeared, placing the Alpine Vision Gran Turismo in Gr.3 recommendations.

The issue was spotted immediately through GT7 experience rather than code review, highlighting the importance of real racing knowledge alongside AI assistance.

The recommendation engine and database were audited, Alpine recommendations were removed pending verification, and a new Report Issue system was introduced to allow future users and Pathfinders to help identify incorrect data.

The deployment was rebuilt and published successfully, with verification confirming the Alpine no longer appears in recommendation results.

Today's lesson:

Trust is built one correction at a time.

No recommendation is above question.

Every improvement strengthens R79 for the racing community.

Snapshot:

* Recommendation integrity improved
* Community reporting system added
* Pathfinder philosophy expanded
* Database audit completed
* Deployment successful
